name: arch-skills
description: Isomorphic architecture-intent documentation and architecture-level review, plus a spec-driven-development conductor, to detect and prevent architectural drift early.
skillInstructions:
Kimi Code tool mapping for arch-skills:

- When a skill says to ask, interview, or wait for the human (arch-why-elicit and arch-why-orchestrate are human-in-the-loop), call Kimi Code's `AskUserQuestion` tool — do not render the questions as plain assistant text.
- When a skill says to dispatch a subagent (arch-doc-orchestrate fans out build/update; arch-spec-flow dispatches arch-spec-review), use Kimi Code's `Agent` tool with a Kimi subagent type (do not pass `general-purpose`); use `subagent_type: "coder"` for filled document/review prompts and `subagent_type: "explore"` for read-only codebase exploration.
- Keep dependent steps sequential; use multiple `Agent` calls, or `run_in_background` only for genuinely independent work.
- When a skill refers to the `Skill` tool, use Kimi Code's native `Skill` tool. Use `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Grep`, `Glob` by their actual exposed names; `Grep` for content search, `Glob` for path search.

interface.shortDescription: Architecture-intent documentation and architecture-level review for coding agents
interface.longDescription: Build isomorphic architecture-intent documents, elicit and ground their rationale, review landed specs against them, and weave both into a spec-driven development flow — to detect and prevent architectural drift early.
